title: Response Commitment, Problem Details, Streaming, and Downloads · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but broken single-owner response and bounded stream protocol; preserve the failing exchange, locate the first causal Node or Express boundary, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in single-owner response and bounded stream protocol and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose status and fields before commitment, assign one response owner, return after terminal sends, and observe writableEnded, headersSent, finish, close, and error distinctly.
Map stable internal failures to RFC 9457 types, status, title, detail, instance, extensions, localization, and disclosure policy.
Control JSON nullability, undefined omission, BigInt, dates, non-finite numbers, property order, cycles, and serialization failure before commitment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Response Commit Single Owner
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team handles case 4162 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose one response shape and stop a producer at the stream backpressure boundary; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose status and fields before commitment, assign one response owner, return after terminal sends, and observe writableEnded, headersSent, finish, close, and error distinctly.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Serve downloads with authorized resource lookup, content disposition, safe filenames, media type, validators, ranges, HEAD parity, byte accounting, and abort cleanup.

hints: For Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Download Range Filename, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Download Range Filename precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Download Range Filename, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug team handles case 5974 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose one response shape and stop a producer at the stream backpressure boundary; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Serve downloads with authorized resource lookup, content disposition, safe filenames, media type, validators, ranges, HEAD parity, byte accounting, and abort cleanup.

For Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Download Range Filename, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: res.download makes any caller-supplied filesystem path and filename safe.
